name: AlphaGo
training_approach: Supervised learning from human expert games, then reinforcement learning via self-play
key_innovations: Combined deep neural networks with Monte Carlo tree search
Learned to play Go at superhuman level — a game thought to be decades away from AI mastery
Self-play reinforcement learning to improve beyond human-level
AlphaGo Zero later achieved superhuman play with zero human knowledge
lineage_path: TD-Gammon → deep reinforcement learning → AlphaGo → AlphaGo Zero → AlphaZero → MuZero
cultural_impact: AlphaGo's 2016 victory over world champion Lee Sedol was a watershed moment for AI. Go has 10^170 possible board positions — brute-force search is impossible. AlphaGo proved that deep learning combined with reinforcement learning could master complex strategic reasoning. The match was watched by 200 million people, sparked massive AI investment in Asia, and demonstrated that neural networks could develop intuition-like capabilities that surprised even expert players with creative, unconventional moves.
